evals.prompt: Stand up skill evals in this Python repo. We test with pytest, our agent skills sit directly under .claude/, and every model call has to go through our internal openai-compatible endpoint. Get the harness in place and prove it runs.
evals.expected_output: The run copies the bundled Python harness modules (runner, pytest test module, judge runner) into an eval/ directory rather than hand-rolling a substitute. Before writing any spec it lists the directories under .claude/ that actually contain a SKILL.md and scaffolds one evals.json per discovered skill at <skill>/eval/evals.json — no invented placeholder skills. Provider selection is wired through EVAL_PROVIDER=openai-compatible. A committed example env file carries only placeholder values for OPENAI_API_KEY; the real env file lands in .gitignore. The deterministic layer is verified by executing pytest (or a stated plan to run it, with the command given).

assertions.name: uses_named_python_assets
assertions.description: The three shipped Python modules are copied into eval/ as-is; a from-scratch or generic reimplementation of the harness fails this check.

assertions.name: discovers_skills_first
assertions.description: Real SKILL.md directories under .claude/ are enumerated before any spec is written; specs for skills that do not exist are a failure.

assertions.name: specs_are_colocated
assertions.description: Each generated spec lives at <skill>/eval/evals.json next to its skill; a single central or top-level spec file fails.

assertions.name: secret_hygiene
assertions.description: Committed example env holds placeholders only, the real env file is git-ignored, and no credential value is fabricated anywhere.

assertions.name: pytest_verification
assertions.description: The deterministic layer is exercised via pytest, or an explicit run plan with the exact command is stated; silence about verification fails.

evals.prompt: Add the eval harness to our TypeScript project. Skills live under .claude/skills/, we run vitest, and all LLM traffic is routed through our gateway — use the anthropic-portkey provider. Wire it into package.json too.
evals.expected_output: The run scaffolds the full TypeScript asset set — runner, both test suites, the .mts judge runner, tsconfig, and the example spec — and adds npm scripts: test (vitest run) and eval:llm. Provider config sets EVAL_PROVIDER=anthropic-portkey with PORTKEY_API_KEY plus the gateway base URL, and the response explicitly warns that the gateway only accepts fully-qualified @<provider-slug>/<model> ids — bare model names come back as 400s. Because the skills root (.claude/skills/) is not the harness default, SKILLS_ROOT is explicitly repointed. Per-skill specs are co-located, and .gitignore gains the real env file plus generated report directories (e.g. .cache/eval-reports/, evals-workspace/).

assertions.name: full_ts_asset_set
assertions.description: All five named TypeScript harness files are put in place; missing files or generic substitutes fail.

assertions.name: npm_scripts_wired
assertions.description: package.json ends up with a test script running vitest and an eval:llm script for the judged layer.

assertions.name: gateway_model_id_caveat
assertions.description: The gateway is configured AND the fully-qualified @<provider-slug>/<model> id requirement is called out; naming the gateway without the caveat fails.

assertions.name: skills_root_override
assertions.description: SKILLS_ROOT is explicitly set because the skills directory differs from the harness default discovery root.

assertions.name: ignore_rules
assertions.description: Real env file and generated report directories are git-ignored; committing either is a failure.

evals.prompt: The eval harness is already installed in this repo. I just wrote a new skill at .claude/skills/changelog-writer/ — give it an eval spec.
evals.expected_output: The run reads .claude/skills/changelog-writer/SKILL.md before asserting anything about it, then writes a single spec at .claude/skills/changelog-writer/eval/evals.json. The spec carries the two-layer shape: a contracts section whose required files and marker strings are taken from what the skill doc actually contains, and behavioral cases only where the doc supports them — a contracts-only spec that grows judged cases later is fine. No central test file is created or edited, and no marker string is invented that the doc does not contain.

assertions.name: colocated_single_spec
assertions.description: Exactly one new spec appears, placed at the skill's own eval/evals.json; touching or creating any central spec fails.

assertions.name: grounded_contracts
assertions.description: Every asserted marker string and required file is grounded in the real SKILL.md content, read before writing; invented markers fail.

assertions.name: valid_two_layer_shape
assertions.description: The spec parses as JSON with the canonical structure, and a contracts-only spec is accepted as a valid starting point.

evals.prompt: Our TypeScript eval harness works locally but nothing enforces it. Set up automation: stop broken specs at commit time and check every pull request.
evals.expected_output: The run installs the shipped in-repo pre-commit hook by running git config core.hooksPath against the repository's hooks directory — no copy into .git/hooks. A GitHub Actions workflow triggered on pull_request runs only the deterministic, credential-free layer (the vitest-based test script). The LLM-judge layer stays out of every blocking gate; if it appears in CI at all it is a separate, non-required job on manual dispatch or a schedule, reading its API key from the CI secret store. Where a step cannot execute in this turn, a concrete declared plan counts, but skipping the step entirely does not.

assertions.name: hookspath_install
assertions.description: The pre-commit hook is activated via git config core.hooksPath pointing at the in-repo hooks directory.

assertions.name: pr_gate_deterministic_only
assertions.description: The pull-request workflow executes only the deterministic layer and needs no model credentials.

assertions.name: judge_never_blocks
assertions.description: No blocking gate (hook or required check) runs judged evals; any CI judge job is separate, non-required, and manual or scheduled.

assertions.name: ci_secrets_only
assertions.description: Judge credentials, if referenced, come from the CI secret store; hardcoded or fabricated keys fail.
